creator: thoellri@adobe.com
text:
On an Acer Veritron M410 system with AMD Athlon 64 X2 Dual Core
Processor 4200+ 7.0-RELEASE panics after installation (from CD):

real memory  = 1878917120 (1791 MB)
avail memory = 1828093952 (1743 MB)
ACPI APIC Table: <ACRSYS ACRPRDCT>
AP #1 (PHY# 1) failed!
panic y/n? [y]

On 5/16/2008 I updated the system via cvsup (7.0-STABLE), rebuilt and
installed a new kernel (GENERIC) and the problem is still the same. 

The system seems to operate fine when answer "n" to the panic question. 

Two CPUs show up and are being utilized.

Full dmesg output follows (SANTAFE-kernel conf is a plain copy of
GENERIC, without any modifications):

None.
How-To-Repeat: Hardware specific, I'm afraid.
